name: Guam (United States)

colors.symbolism: Guam's union with the United States and the Pacific Ocean surrounding the island
the blood shed by the Chamorro people under Spanish, Japanese and other occupations
the sandy beach at Hagåtña depicted in the seal
the coconut palm in the seal
the proa (Chamorro outrigger canoe) and palm trunk in the seal

description: A dark blue field with a narrow red border, bearing in the centre an almond-shaped seal of Guam: a Chamorro outrigger canoe sailing in Hagåtña Bay with a coconut palm in the foreground.
history: The Great Seal of Guam was designed by Helen L. Paul, wife of a U.S. Navy officer, in 1917. The seal was placed on a blue flag in 1930 and the red border was added in 1948 by act of the Guam Legislature in commemoration of those who died during the Japanese occupation of 1941–1944.

features.symbolism: a dark blue field bordered narrowly in red — the red border was added during World War II to commemorate the bloodshed of the Chamorro people during the Japanese occupation

an almond-shaped (mandorla) seal showing a flying proa (a Chamorro outrigger canoe) sailing in Hagåtña Bay, with a coconut palm in the foreground and the cliffs of Two Lovers' Point in the background, around the inscription 'GUAM' — the proa and palm reflect Chamorro maritime culture and resilience
